title: pilosella tricheilema
intro:
Pilosella tricheilema, also known as mouse-ear hawkweed or false mouse-ear hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to mountainous regions of Central Europe and parts of Asia.

This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities.

The health benefits of Pilosella tricheilema include its ability to help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and gastrointestinal disorders.

The plant's medicinal properties can be utilized through various herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used both internally and externally to support overall health and well-being.
